title: ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY - 211info
text: GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES ALCOHOL AND DRUG COUNSELING AND ADVOCACY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers mental health and substance abuse assessments. Specialists provide consultations, referrals, support and resources to staff, and group therapy, such as Seeking Safety. All consultations and assessments are confidential, except when there's a risk of harm to oneself or others, or in cases of child, disabled person, or elder abuse. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing the impacts of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assult Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 988-6400 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or services. No walk-ins. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet MAX Blue line and buses 15, 20, 287 Services: Alcohol Use Disorder Support Groups * Domestic Violence Issues Assessment for Substance Use Disorders * Domestic Violence Issues Drug Use Disorder Support Groups * Domestic Violence Issues Mental Health Evaluation * Domestic Violence Issues Substance Use Disorder Counseling * Domestic Violence Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: INFORMATION SERVICES - INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT LEGAL SERVICES - LEGAL AID SERVICES OF OREGON INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE LEGAL SERVICES - PROSECUTION FOR SURVIVORS PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SELF-SUFFICIENCY SERVICES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES
